items.headline: Claude Cowork is leaving your desktop, now works from your phone too
items.summary: Anthropic announced on July 7 that Claude Cowork, its AI agent for ongoing multi-step tasks, is expanding beyond desktop to web browsers and iOS/Android apps, starting in beta for Max plan subscribers. You can kick off a task on your laptop, let it keep working in the background even after you close the app, and get a notification on your phone when it needs a decision or finishes.
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic's own usage data shows most Cowork sessions aren't coding at all, business tasks and content drafts make up about half of all use, so if you've been curious about letting an AI agent handle research or first-draft writing, this is aimed at exactly that kind of work, and soon you'll be able to check on it from your phone instead of staying chained to your desk.

opportunity.angle: I let Claude Cowork keep working on a task while I was out filming and checked in from my phone, here's how it went

items.headline: OpenAI's most powerful model yet starts rolling out to everyone this week
items.summary: OpenAI is expanding GPT-5.6 worldwide starting July 9, after the U.S. government had asked it to limit access to a small group of partners while extra safety testing ran. The lineup has a flagship version called Sol plus two smaller, cheaper versions, Terra and Luna, aimed at faster or lower-cost use.
items.why_it_matters: If you use ChatGPT for research, writing, or brainstorming video ideas, expect a noticeably smarter option to show up in your account this week. Try the cheaper Terra or Luna tiers first before assuming you need the priciest one.

items.headline: Elon Musk's xAI is officially gone, folded into SpaceX as "SpaceXAI"
items.summary: The @SpaceXAI account went live on X on July 6, completing a rebrand first announced back in May: xAI, the maker of Grok, is no longer a separate company and is now fully part of SpaceX. The products you'd actually use, Grok, Grok Voice, Grok Imagine, and the X app, aren't changing names, just the corporate structure behind them.
items.why_it_matters: This is mostly a business-structure story, not a feature change, but if you use Grok or post on X, don't be surprised to see "SpaceXAI" branding start showing up around the edges.

categories.title: Tools & Tips for Creators and Small Biz

items.headline: Meta's new AI image tool can restyle your product photos, but it can also edit other people's public Instagram photos
items.summary: Meta launched Muse Image, its first in-house AI image generator, free inside the Meta AI app, Instagram, and WhatsApp on July 7. It handles detailed, multi-step prompts, like restyling a room, doing product photography, or turning a selfie into a Renaissance-style portrait, and it can also edit photos that other Instagram users have posted publicly.
items.why_it_matters: This is a free way to whip up product shots or stylized graphics without hiring a designer, but that last feature, editing other people's public photos, is also a reason to double-check your own Instagram privacy settings if you'd rather your posts not be fair game for strangers' edits.

opportunity.angle: I tested Meta's new free AI image tool for product shots so you don't have to
